headlines: Drift Protocol hit by huge Solana exploit
Circle criticized for not freezing USDC after Drift theft
Ethereum Foundation stakes ~45,000 ETH, nears 70,000 ETH goal
U.S. (CFTC & DOJ) sues states over prediction-market rules

DeFi hacks fall in Q1 but big single losses persist ($169M)  : Q1 2026 update from Cointelegraph','Coinbase wins conditional OCC national trust charter approval','BTC vs ETH ETF flows diverge (BTC inflows, ETH outflows) — weekly data','Riot sells 3,778 BTC in Q1 2026 — miner treasury moves

articles.title: Drift Protocol suffers massive exploit on Solana (≈$270–285M)
articles.summary: Drift, a major Solana-based perpetuals exchange, confirmed an active attack on April 1–2, 2026. On‑chain analysts and security firms estimated $270–$285 million was drained; the protocol paused deposits and withdrawals while investigators traced funds across chains. This is one of 2026’s largest DeFi losses and pushed SOL and many Solana projects lower.

articles.title: Circle faces criticism for not freezing stolen USDC after Drift hack
articles.summary: Investigators say large amounts of stolen assets were converted into USDC and bridged to Ethereum. Critics — including blockchain sleuths — argue Circle could have acted faster to block or blacklist addresses; Circle says it only freezes funds when legally required or at law‑enforcement request.
articles.source: CoinDesk coverage cited by PYMNTS / MoneyCheck

articles.title: Ethereum Foundation stakes ~45,034 ETH, nears 70,000 ETH target
articles.summary: On April 3, 2026 the Ethereum Foundation moved roughly 45,034 ETH into the Beacon Chain in multiple equal deposits, bringing its total staked ETH close to the 70,000‑ETH goal it announced earlier. The move locks more ETH into consensus staking and slightly reduces liquid supply available on exchanges.

articles.title: CFTC and DOJ sue Illinois, Arizona and Connecticut over prediction‑market regulation
articles.summary: On April 2–3, 2026 the U.S. government (CFTC backed by DOJ filings) sued several states to stop state gambling authorities from enforcing cease‑and‑desist orders against federally‑regulated prediction markets. The cases raise a big jurisdiction fight over whether event contracts are federally regulated derivatives or state‑level gambling.

articles.title: DeFi hacks Q1 2026: ~$169M lost, decline from prior year but single exploits still large
articles.summary: Quarterly tracking shows DeFi exploits stole about $169M in Q1 2026 across dozens of incidents — a big year‑on‑year drop from 2025 but still driven by a few very large attacks (including the early‑April Drift event). Security remains a top risk for decentralized finance.

articles.title: Coinbase gets conditional OCC national trust charter (step toward federal custody status)
articles.summary: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted Coinbase conditional approval to form a national trust company for custody and market‑infrastructure services. This is not a final bank license but it moves Coinbase closer to a single federal regulatory home for custody services and institutional products.

articles.title: ETF flows: Bitcoin ETFs net inflows while Ether ETFs show outflows (week of Mar 30–Apr 2)
articles.summary: Weekly data show continued net inflows into U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs while Ether‑focused ETFs saw net outflows over the same short window. The divergence matters for price leadership and short‑term liquidity across BTC vs ETH markets.

articles.title: Riot Platforms sold 3,778 BTC in Q1 2026 (miner treasury movement)
articles.summary: Riot disclosed the sale of 3,778 BTC in Q1 2026, raising roughly $289.5M in proceeds. Miner sales like this can influence short‑term BTC supply on exchanges and are monitored by traders as a liquidity and price factor.
